element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic - enables follower movement and settlement on flat land
Population growth tied to available flat land; larger land = larger houses
Mana accumulates as followers build houses; unlocks divine powers
Offensive divine power to disrupt enemy followers
Offensive divine power affecting terrain and population
Destructive divine power requiring careful placement
Convert followers to powerful combat units for final conflict
Present from start; victory requires eliminating enemy population

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully flattening terrain for new settlements
Mana accumulation enabling new divine powers
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual escalation with power unlocks; early levels emphasize expansion and resource accumulation, mid-levels introduce offensive capabilities, late levels force confrontation through endgame mechanic

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like: learning terrain manipulation and house-building mechanics with minimal enemy pressure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating: managing multiple settlements while enemy grows; introducing divine powers that unlock progressively
difficulty_curve.late_game: Confrontational: forced endgame battle where player must convert followers to knights and achieve population dominance
notable_spikes: Introduction of offensive divine powers (earthquakes, floods)
Endgame forced conflict mechanic requiring population advantage

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level features different terrain types (desert, rock/lava, snow/ice, etc.) that are not purely aesthetic - they materially affect how player and enemy followers develop and expand
Divine powers unlock sequentially as mana accumulates from follower house-building, creating natural progression gates and pacing mechanism across 500 levels
Victory path requires balancing land-raising for own settlements while strategically removing/modifying enemy terrain; foundational to all levels
